sanskrit:
सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः।
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम्॥
transliteration:
sarve ’py ete yajña-vido yajña-kṣapita-kalmaṣāḥ
yajña-śiṣṭāmṛta-bhujo yānti brahma sanātanam
literal_meaning: All these sacrificers, knowing the purpose of sacrifice, having their sins dissolved by sacrifice, tasting the nectar of sacrifice’s remnants, attain the eternal Absolute.
contextual_meaning: Any disciplined, values-based action—when done mindfully and without clinging—purges guilt, nourishes the psyche, and opens a felt sense of timeless, boundless presence.
psychological_state: Post-ritual clarity: intrusive self-criticism quiets, the mind feels light, and the self-sense expands beyond clock time into an 'eternal now.'
trigger_scenario: After a week of nightly digital-detox journaling, you notice shame about past mistakes has softened and you wake up feeling spacious and 'clean.'
anchor_text: Give your best, let go of the outcome, feel peace.
